name: Muslim Community Network
mission: We are dedicated to using civic education and leadership development to shape the public narrative about what it means to be Muslim in America.

geographicCoverage: New York City (New York, United States)
Brooklyn (Kings County, New York, United States)

theoryOfChange: Equipping community leaders and frontline staff with cultural competency and safety tools leads to safer, stronger, and more civically engaged communities.
scoreSummary: MCN shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mcnny.org/donate (Source: https://mcnny.org/donate)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 6.9% of revenue ($82,006 on $1,193,572)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.

score_details.score_summary: MCN shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mcnny.org/donate (Source: https://mcnny.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: MCN reported total revenue of $1,193,572 and a program expense ratio of 72.9%.
The organization is zakat-eligible and provides information on zakat policy.
MCN offers various donation options and giving methods for supporters.
MCN holds a Charity Navigator score of 98.0/100, with 8.2 months of working capital and a fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: MCN demonstrates strong financial health and transparency, though its overall score is moderated by its focus on capacity-building which is harder to measure for direct impact compared to service-delivery models.
areas_for_improvement: Limited external evidence regarding long-term cost-effectiveness per beneficiary
Opportunity to increase the program expense ratio to further direct more resources toward core services

dimension_explanations.alignment: MCN offers a medium donor fit for those interested in religious and cultural advocacy, specifically within the American Muslim context [2]. Its mission addresses the urgent need for civic representation, supported by a total revenue of $1,193,572 to fund its community-centric initiatives [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through youth and civic programs, maintaining a program expense ratio of 72.9% [1][4]. While it shows high financial health with 8.2 months of working capital, specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not currently available to fully quantify its direct effectiveness [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: MCN empowers the American Muslim community through civic education and leadership development to reshape the public narrative.
strengths: High financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 98.0/100
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.03 to raise every $1
Robust liquidity with 8.2 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: MCN is a New York-based organization dedicated to shaping the public perception of Muslims in America through youth leadership, community education, and civic engagement [1][4]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various giving methods [2][3].

all_citations.quote: MCN is Zakat eligible... 100% of your Zakat goes directly to those in need.

targeted programs in the Bronx, Harlem, and Queens

complete a BBB Wise Giving Alliance accreditation

areas_for_improvement.context: While MCN tracks outcomes, there is <cite id="6">limited external evaluation</cite> available to verify long-term cost-effectiveness per beneficiary.
For an organization of this size, <cite id="1">CEO compensation represents over 10% of total revenue</cite>, which may warrant closer board scrutiny.

bbb_assessment.summary: MCN has not yet completed a formal BBB Wise Giving Alliance review.

case_against.mitigation_notes: MCN is actively sharing feedback practices and outcome metrics through Candid to improve transparency.

<cite id="1">CEO pay exceeds 10% of total annual revenue</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="6">lack of third-party impact data</cite> and a relatively high executive pay-to-revenue ratio.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and transparency data available.

alignment.explanation: MCN is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> across <cite id="2">eight traditional asnaf categories</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="2">clarify its specific zakat allocation policy</cite> to provide donors more detail on how funds are distributed among the needy.

credibility.explanation: MCN is highly credible, evidenced by an <cite id="4">independent board that is 83% autonomous</cite> and a <cite id="6">Platinum Candid Seal</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further strengthen credibility, MCN could <cite id="7">complete a BBB Wise Giving Alliance accreditation</cite> to meet all 20 standards of accountability.

impact.explanation: The organization is efficient, with an <cite id="4">72.9% program ratio</cite> and <cite id="4">resilient working capital of 13 months</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact reporting would improve by <cite id="6">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-per-beneficiary analysis.

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Direct service delivery rather than grantmaking

rich_narrative.headline: MCN empowers the American Muslim community through civic education and leadership development to reshape the public narrative.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to empower the American Muslim identity through civic participation and local New York City community support.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through a locally-focused NYC organization
Believe in the importance of Muslim representation in the public narrative
Value high transparency and top-tier Charity Navigator ratings
Support youth leadership and immigration assistance
giving_considerations: Donations support a mix of direct aid (Zakat) and advocacy/education
The organization is mid-sized, meaning individual gifts have high visibility
Focus is strictly on the New York City metropolitan area
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international humanitarian relief or those who require rigorous academic impact evaluations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that the organization <cite id="6">tracks 3 outcome metrics</cite> but lacks independent third-party evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MCN uses civic education and leadership training to empower Muslims to advocate for justice and reshape public perception.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that civic training actually leads to increased community engagement and policy influence.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="1">revenue growth of 10.9%</cite>, MCN is expanding but could absorb more to scale its Impact Academy.
strategic_priorities: Increase civic engagement in underrepresented boroughs

peer_comparison.differentiator: MCN stands out with a <cite id="4">98.0/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite>, which is higher than many local grassroots peers.

strengths.detail: MCN holds a <cite id="4">98/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, supported by a <cite id="4">perfect 100 score in Culture & Accountability</cite>.
The organization demonstrates remarkable efficiency, spending only <cite id="4">$0.05 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
MCN maintains <cite id="4">8.2 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational stability.

strengths_deep_dive: MCN's financial health is a primary differentiator. With <cite id="1">$768,698 in annual revenue</cite>, the organization manages to keep overhead low while maximizing community output. Their <cite id="4">72.9% program expense ratio</cite> ensures that the vast majority of donor funds directly support initiatives like the MCN Impact Academy and the Food Drive. Furthermore, the organization has achieved the <cite id="6">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of recognition for non-profit data sharing.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Network (MCN) serves as a vital civic anchor for New York City's diverse Muslim population. The organization focuses on <cite id="1">shaping the public narrative about Muslims in America</cite> through leadership development and civic education. MCN operates with a high degree of financial integrity, maintaining an <cite id="4">overall score of 98.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. This rating reflects a strong commitment to transparency and governance. Financially, the organization reported <cite id="1">total revenue of $768,698</cite> in the 2023 tax year. While maintaining a lean operation, they ensure that <cite id="4">72.9% of expenses are directed toward programs</cite>, which is significantly higher than many peer non-profits. Their work spans across the five boroughs, including <cite id="5">targeted programs in the Bronx, Harlem, and Queens</cite>. By providing platforms for youth and community members to engage in the democratic process, MCN addresses the systemic need for Muslim representation in the public square. The organization also manages a <cite id="2">dedicated Zakat Fund</cite> to support vulnerable community members, ensuring that religious obligations are met with local impact.
